options.name: --ttl
options.description: Minutes to live for a SSH session

options.name: --insecure
options.description: Do not verify server's certificate and host name. Use only in test environments

options.name: --auth
options.description: Specify the name of authentication connector to use

options.name: --skip-version-check
options.description: Skip version checking between server and client

name: -k
--add-keys-to-agent
options.description: Controls how keys are handled. Valid values are [auto no yes only]

options.name: --enable-escape-sequences
options.description: Enable support for SSH escape sequences. Type '~?' during an SSH session to list supported sequences. Default is enabled

options.name: --bind-addr
options.description: Override host:port used when opening a browser for cluster logins

subcommands.name: ssh
subcommands.description: Run shell or execute a command on a remote SSH node

args.name: user@hostname
args.description: Address of remote machine to log into

options.description: Forces ssh to try protocol version 1 only
Forces ssh to try protocol version 2 only
Forces ssh to use IPv4 addresses only
Forces ssh to use IPv6 addresses only

options.name: -A
options.description: Enables forwarding of the authentication agent connection

options.name: -a
options.description: Disables forwarding of the authentication agent connection

options.name: -b
options.description: Use bind_address on the local machine as the source address of the connection

options.name: -C
options.description: Requests compression of all data (including stdin, stdout, stderr, and data for forwarded X11 and TCP connections)

options.name: -c
options.description: Selects the cipher specification for encrypting the session

options.name: -D
options.description: Specifies a local 'dynamic' application-level port forwarding

options.name: -e
options.description: Sets the escape character for sessions with a pty (default: '~')

options.name: -F
options.description: Specifies an alternative per-user configuration file

options.name: -f
options.description: Requests ssh to go to background just before command execution

options.name: -g
options.description: Allows remote hosts to connect to local forwarded ports

options.name: -I
options.description: Specify the PKCS#11 shared library ssh should use to communicate with a PKCS#11 token providing the user's private RSA key

options.name: -i
options.description: Selects a file from which the identity (private key) for public key authentication is read

options.name: -K
options.description: Enables GSSAPI-based authentication and forwarding (delegation) of GSSAPI credentials to the server

options.name: -k
options.description: Disables forwarding (delegation) of GSSAPI credentials to the server

options.name: -L
options.description: Specifies that the given port on the local (client) host is to be forwarded to the given host and port on the remote side

args.name: port:host:hostport
args.description: Local port, followed by host and host port to forward to

options.name: -l
options.description: Specifies the user to log in as on the remote machine

args.name: login name
args.description: Name of user logging into remote machine

options.name: -M
options.description: Places the ssh client into ``master'' mode for connection sharing

options.name: -m
options.description: Additionally, for protocol version 2 a comma-separated list of MAC (message authentication code) algorithms can be specified in order of preference

options.name: -N
options.description: Do not execute a remote command

options.name: -n
options.description: Redirects stdin from /dev/null (actually, prevents reading from stdin)

options.name: -O
options.description: Control an active connection multiplexing master process

args.name: ctl cmd
args.description: Command that's passed to the master process

options.name: -o
options.description: Can be used to give options in the format used in the configuration file

args.name: option
args.description: Options that can be specified in the format of the config file

options.name: -p
options.description: Port to connect to on the remote host

options.name: -q
options.description: Quiet mode.  Causes most warning and diagnostic messages to be suppressed

options.name: -R
options.description: Specifies that the given port on the remote (server) host is to be forwarded to the given host and port on the local side

args.name: port:host:hostport
args.description: Local port, followed by host and host port to forward to

options.name: -S
options.description: Specifies the location of a control socket for connection sharing, or the string 'none' to disable connection sharing

options.name: -s
options.description: May be used to request invocation of a subsystem on the remote system

options.name: -V
options.description: Display the version number and exit

options.name: -v
options.description: Verbose mode.  Causes ssh to print debugging messages about its progress

options.name: -W
options.description: Requests that standard input and output on the client be forwarded to host on port over the secure channel

args.name: host:port
args.description: Host and port to forward to

options.name: -w
options.description: Requests tunnel device forwarding with the specified tun(4) devices between the client (local_tun) and the server (remote_tun)

options.name: -y
options.description: Send log information using the syslog(3) system module

options.name: --ttl
options.description: Minutes to live for a SSH session

options.name: --insecure
options.description: Do not verify server's certificate and host name. Use only in test environments

options.name: --auth
options.description: Specify the name of authentication connector to use

options.name: --skip-version-check
options.description: Skip version checking between server and client

name: -k
--add-keys-to-agent
options.description: Controls how keys are handled. Valid values are [auto no yes only]

options.name: --enable-escape-sequences
options.description: Enable support for SSH escape sequences. Type '~?' during an SSH session to list supported sequences. Default is enabled

options.name: --bind-addr
options.description: Override host:port used when opening a browser for cluster logins

--add-keys-to-agent
